summary: Round-1 items (1), (2), and (4) appear fixed in the reviewed slice, but item (3) is not fully enforced: the internal token is compared timing-safely, yet the code never verifies that the backing token file is actually mode 0600 before accepting it.

findings.title: Internal token trust boundary is not enforced by file mode
findings.recommendation: Before caching or comparing the token, `stat` the token file and reject or repair any file whose permissions are broader than `0o600` (and ideally reject symlinks as well). As written, any pre-existing world/group-readable token file is still accepted, so the auth-env route does not actually require a 0600 token file to guard secret access.
